sections.title: Other styles
sections.text: Any other presentation of the product shall be permitted provided that the product: (a) is sufficiently distinctive from other forms of presentation laid down in this standard; (b) meets all relevant requirements of this standard, including requirements relating to limitations on defects, drained weight, and any other requirements in this standard which are applicable to that style in the standard which most closely resembles the style, or styles intended to be provided for under this provision; and (c) is adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text:
4.1 It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in 
accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969),2 and other 
codes of practice recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission which are relevant to this product. 
4.2 To the extent possible in good manufacturing practice, the product shall be free from objectionable matter. 
4.3 When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product: 
- shall be free from microorganisms in amounts which may represent a hazard to health; 
- shall be free from parasites which may represent a hazard to health; and 
- shall not contain any substance originating from microorganisms in amounts which may represent a 
hazard to health.

HYGIENE
number=4
level=1
4.1 It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in 
accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969),2 and other 
codes of practice recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission which are relevant to this product. 
4.2 To the extent possible in good manufacturing practice, the product shall be free from objectionable matter. 
4.3 When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product: 
- shall be free from microorganisms in amounts which may represent a hazard to health; 
- shall be free from parasites which may represent a hazard to health; and 
- shall not contain any substance originating from microorganisms in amounts which may represent a 
hazard to health.

sections.title: Within the tolerance provided for in subsection 3.2.1, the following individual tolerances shall apply:
path_titles: Within the tolerance provided for in subsection 3.2.1, the following individual tolerances shall apply:
sections.text:
Defects     Tolerance 
 mineral impurities    not more than 2% m/m 
 organic impurities of vegetable origin  not more than 0.02% m/m except for shiitake 
       mushrooms for which the maximum  
       shall be 1% m/m 
 maggot-damaged fungi: 
 wild-growing fungi    not more than 20% m/m of total damage   
       including serious damage 
 cultivated fungi     not more than 1% m/m of total damage including 
       not more than 0.5% m/m of serious damage 
 crushed fungi     not more than 6% m/m 
 carbonized fungi     not more than 2% m/m 
 damaged fungi     not more than 20% m/m 
fallen-off stalks shall be equal in number to caps, i.e. 1:1.

pages.summary: Página contém título principal do documento e cabeçalho de página, sem seções ou texto interno adicional.
Página com cabeçalho da norma e duas seções principais não numeradas em caixa alta: 2022 AMENDMENTS e 2023 AMENDMENTS, cada uma acompanhada de texto explicativo. Não há continuação entre páginas.

Página com cabeçalho identificando a norma e seu número. Identificadas três seções principais numeradas: 1 SCOPE, 2 DESCRIPTION, 3 ESSENTIAL QUALITY FACTORS. Seção 2 contém subtítulos e subsubtítulos numerados claramente. Texto associado corretamente a cada nó. Não há continuidade entre páginas.

A página apresenta conteúdo com continuidade de seção 3 da página anterior, incluindo subseções 3.2.2, 3.3 com subitens 3.3.1 e 3.3.2. Em seguida, inicia a seção 4 HYGIENE e a seção 5 PACKAGING AND PRESENTATION com seus subtítulos 5.1, 5.2 e 5.3. Os títulos estão numéricos e claros, facilitando a identificação da hierarquia. Textos associados aos respectivos títulos conforme o conteúdo. Cabeçalho identificado como 'CXS 39-1981 5'. Continuidade marcada na seção 3.2.2 e no grupo 3. Seções 4 e 5 completas na página, sem continuidade para outras páginas.

A página contém as seções 6 LABELLING (com subseções 6.1 e seus subitens 6.1.1 a 6.1.4, e subseção 6.2) e 7 METHODS OF ANALYSIS AND SAMPLING. A seção 6 é continuação da página anterior. O texto está associado corretamente aos respectivos títulos. O cabeçalho é identificado como 'CXS 39-1981 6'. Não há continuidade para próximas páginas.
